docs.title: Helakit
docs.text:
A toolkit for validating and working with Sri Lankan data.

Helakit is a small, dependency-free Python library for validating Sri Lankan identifiers — NIC numbers, phone numbers, postal codes, and more.

from helakit import validate_nic

result = validate_nic("199012345678")
if result.is_valid:
    print(result.data["dob"], result.data["gender"])

See Getting Started to install and try it, or jump to a specific validator:

  • NIC
  • Phone
  • Postal

Alpha software

Helakit is pre-1.0. The API may change before the first stable release.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text: • Initial scaffolding: package layout, validator stubs (NIC, phone, postal), ValidationResult core, docs site, CI / docs / release workflows, and development tooling (ruff, mypy, pytest, pre-commit).

docs.title: Contributing to Helakit
docs.text: Thanks for your interest in helakit! This page walks through setting up a local dev environment, running the checks, and submitting a PR.

docs.title: Dev environment
docs.text:
Helakit uses uv for project and dependency management. Once uv is installed:

uv sync --all-extras       # create a venv and install dev + docs extras
uv run pre-commit install  # wire up the git pre-commit hook

Everything below assumes you're running through uv run, which uses the project's pinned environment.

docs.title: Running the checks
docs.text:
uv run pytest                    # tests + coverage
uv run ruff check .              # lint
uv run ruff format --check .     # formatting
uv run mypy src                  # type check (strict)
uv run pre-commit run --all-files

To preview the docs locally:

uv run mkdocs serve

docs.title: Pull request checklist
docs.text:
Before opening a PR, please make sure:

  • [ ] Tests are added or updated for any behaviour change.
  • [ ] Docs are updated for anything user-visible.
  • [ ] An entry has been added to CHANGELOG.md under [Unreleased].
  • [ ] ruff check, ruff format --check, mypy src, and pytest all pass.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
Docs are published in three flavours, courtesy of mike:

  • <site>/dev/ — built from every push to main. Always reflects in-progress work and may be ahead of the latest release.
  • <site>/<MAJOR>.<MINOR>/ — built from each release tag (v0.1.0, v0.2.0, …). Versioned, frozen.
  • <site>/latest/ — alias that always points at the most recent release. The bare site URL redirects here.

Visitors hitting the bare site URL land on /latest/ by default, so the homepage always shows the newest released version.

docs.title: Data contributions are welcome
docs.text: A lot of helakit's value comes from accurate lookup tables — postal codes, mobile prefixes, district names, and so on. Adding entries (or correcting existing ones) is one of the easiest ways to contribute and doesn't require deep Python knowledge: each table is a plain dict in src/helakit/_data/. Please cite a source in the PR description so we can verify the data.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install helakit

Helakit supports Python 3.10 and newer and has no runtime dependencies.

docs.title: Two ways to validate
docs.text: Every validator ships in two flavours: a rich one that returns a ValidationResult, and a boolean shorthand.

docs.title: Rich result (recommended)
docs.text:
from helakit import validate_nic

result = validate_nic("199012345678")
if result.is_valid:
    print(result.normalized)
    print(result.data["dob"], result.data["gender"])
else:
    for err in result.errors:
        print(err.code, err.message)

ValidationResult is truthy when valid, so plain if validate_nic(x): also works.

docs.title: Boolean shorthand
docs.text:
from helakit import is_valid_nic

is_valid_nic("199012345678")  # True

Use the rich form when you need parsed fields or precise error reporting, and the shorthand when you only need a yes/no.

docs.title: SUMMARY
docs.text:
• helakit
  • helakit.nic
  • helakit.phone
  • helakit.postal

docs.title: helakit
docs.text: Helakit — a toolkit for validating and working with Sri Lankan data.

docs.title: HelakitError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Base class for every exception raised by helakit.

docs.title: InvalidInputError
docs.text:
Bases: HelakitError

Raised when an input is the wrong type or otherwise unusable.

This signals a programmer error, not a validation failure. A malformed NIC string returns a :class:ValidationResult with is_valid=False; passing None instead of a string raises this.

docs.title: ValidationError dataclass
docs.text:
ValidationError(code: str, message: str, field: str | None = None)

A single validation failure.

Attributes:

Name Type Description code str

A short machine-readable identifier (e.g. "nic.bad_checksum").

message str

A human-readable description of what went wrong.

field str | None

The specific field within the input that failed, if applicable.

docs.title: ValidationResult dataclass
docs.text:
ValidationResult(is_valid: bool, value: str, normalized: str | None = None, errors: list[ValidationError] = list(), data: dict[str, Any] = dict())

The outcome of validating a single value.

Attributes:

Name Type Description is_valid bool

True if the input passed every check.

value str

The original input string, unmodified.

normalized str | None

The canonical representation of value when valid.

errors list[ValidationError]

Every error encountered. Empty when is_valid is True.

data dict[str, Any]

Structured fields extracted during validation (e.g. NIC date of birth, gender). Empty when no fields could be extracted.

required

class:ValidationResult with parsed fields (date of birth,

gender, voting eligibility, …) populated when valid.

docs.title: NIC
docs.text:
Sri Lanka issues a National Identity Card number that encodes the holder's date of birth, gender, and (in the old format) voting eligibility.

Coming soon

NIC validation is the next thing to land. Until then, calling validate_nic raises NotImplementedError.

docs.title: Planned API
docs.text:
from helakit import validate_nic, is_valid_nic

result = validate_nic("199012345678")
result.data       # {"dob": date(1990, 5, 4), "gender": "male", ...}
result.normalized # "199012345678"

is_valid_nic("199012345678")  # True

Both old (901234567V) and new (199012345678) formats will be supported.

docs.title: Phone
docs.text:
Sri Lankan phone numbers come in local (0712345678) and international (+94712345678) forms, and the leading prefix identifies the carrier or whether the number is mobile or fixed-line.

Coming soon

Phone validation is planned. Until then, validate_phone raises NotImplementedError.

docs.title: Planned API
docs.text:
from helakit import validate_phone, is_valid_phone

result = validate_phone("+94712345678")
result.normalized  # "+94712345678"
result.data        # {"type": "mobile", "carrier": "Mobitel", ...}

is_valid_phone("0712345678")  # True

docs.title: Postal
docs.text:
Sri Lankan postal codes are five digits, with each code mapping to a specific post office, district, and province.

Coming soon

Postal-code validation is planned. Until then, validate_postal raises NotImplementedError.

docs.title: Planned API
docs.text:
from helakit import validate_postal, is_valid_postal

result = validate_postal("10100")
result.data  # {"district": "Colombo", "province": "Western", ...}

is_valid_postal("10100")  # True
